author: @Deborah-c9n9e
text: What a great interview!! I love Jill's forward-looking attitude toward life. She has four children; I was unable to have any. However, as a widow still living out in the sticks on a handful of acres, I am constantly thinking about how I can improve the food production here. This includes recently planting more fruit trees and grape vines, adding raised beds, and the like. And why do I ultimately choose to do this? Yes, Yah-willing, I will reap many benefits, but I also think much about the NEXT family who will occupy this home and enjoy the "fruits" of this little slice of heaven!!

author: @wherethereisloveblog
text: Tried and true sticker removal - use 1-5 drops of olive oil, veggie oil, or any oil in your kitchen probably will work. The sticker will come off and any remaining glue will be easy to remove with a paper towel. You only need a few drops of oil, any more might make a mess or become rancid if not cleaned off. A tiny amount on paper towel easily breaks down the glue bond.

I have a great Dollar Tree tip… Those old and nasty coffee and tea cups, they have that “gunky brown” stain on the sides of the cup… this is how you fix that quickly using a cheap Dollar Tree chemical.  Give the inside of the cup 3 to 4 sprays of full strength Awesome yellow cleaner, wait 30 seconds, use a sponge or cloth under the faucet to wipe out the “grunge”. After many attempts in the dishwasher and using a variety of chemicals, I found this to be quick, easy, and most importantly cheap!

author: @aliciaboyd
text: About a year ago, someone trademarked the term “tubie/tuby” when referring to feeding tubes and their accessories. Things like “I’m a tubie” for someone with a feeding tube, “tubie tape” or “tubie supplies,” etc. She did not come up the term and many people/companies/shops used it long before she herself became a “tubie/tuby” patient. For a short time, small businesses on Etsy and other similar platforms were shutdown and/or forced to stop using the term, but the backlash was swift and strong from those in the “tubie community.” Unsurprisingly, she quickly withdrew the trademark, but I believe that it had already harmed her name and small business. I’m guessing that this “hot girls read” scandal will end in a similar fashion.
video_title: A Greedy Etsy Seller Trademarks BookToks “Hot-Girls-Read” And The Community Loses Its Mind

Heavenly Father…

We come to you with hearts full of compassion, lifting up this precious mother and her incredible and wonderful daughter Tiffany. 

Lord, we ask that you wrap both of them in your loving arms during this incredibly difficult journey.

For Tiffany: We pray for complete healing, strength, and comfort. Guide the hands of her medical team, grant her body the resilience to endure the new treatment plan, and ease any pain, anxiety, or fear she may be feeling.

For Amma: We pray for a special measure of your peace as she walks this path beside her daughter and give her the endurance to be a pillar of strength, while also providing her with moments of rest and comfort when she feels helpless or overwhelmed. Remind her that she is never alone. Fill her heart with unwavering hope and the courage to face each new day.

Surround them both with a loving support system, a calming presence, and the assurance of your unending love in your holy and comforting name we pray. AMEN! 🙏

author: @laylou1985
text: She should be ok to go to the bathroom in the backyard! High traffic dog/animal areas are more of a concern. Puppy pads are good in a pinch, but it’s hard to break them from going inside if they are consistently be used :)

author: @mitchellgames06
text: As long as sunny has her vaccines and you’re not leaving her alone outside, she should be fine using the bathroom in the yard. The yard is better and will help with potty training, Also for my dog when she was a puppy after she used the bathroom i always gave her a treat and that really worked for her training so maybe that can help with stopping her peeing on your bed lol. My dogs 6 years old now, she doesn’t use any pads in the house nor does she have accidents in the house. The teething phase is like their terrible twos 😅 it had me rethinking my life lol but all you gotta do is pull through and get LOTS of toys🙏 you got this.

author: @HannahKnaack-c4s
text: RuthAnn, you are such a jewel in the garden of life-- the number of positive comments on your channel is amazing. I'm fairly new to YouTube and have been searching for videos done by "authentic" people-- you and Elvin certainly caught my eye. The number of things that kept me coming back began to add up, lol. When you said, "Iowa" -- our family's favorite location-- I was reminded of all the trips we took on the eastern side of the state in the ten years we lived there. We actively sought out Amish stores (and even an Amish cheese factory), something we've done in the northern states we've lived in. My widowed paternal grandmother married a man who was Old Order German Baptist (Dayton, OH area), so I'm familiar to a degree with much of what you've spoken of in interviews. I have the fondest memories of her water pump just inside her screen door and the tin cup we drank from--and the time she took us for a ride in the buggy, although she did keep Hazel to a very slow "clip-clop"! Your bubbly personality reminds me so much of my mother, who woke each morning looking for joy in the day and loved her Lord, heart and soul. Widowed three times, she still kept focused on her True North. In summer, she canned anything that moved, with us four sisters snapping bushels of beans and shelling peas. We picked dark, sweet cherries as a family (my favorite) and canned them quickly but the kitchen looked like it had chicken pox by the time we were done, haha! My husband and I enjoy Elvin's many talents and the way he patiently teaches the children. You both show what's most important in raising responsible children and make no apologies for a parenting style that is so refreshingly different from today's society. I caught a quote recently that I'll share as it applies to what attracts people to your channel: "People aren't longing to be impressed; they're longing to feel like they're home". You do "home" so well, RuthAnn. (P.S. --we have the twin to the gray sectional sofa in your living room--too funny.)

author: @buckonono7996
text: Good morning RuthAnn!  I love cattle panels too.  We live intown, but grow what we are able.  When we put in 2x7 ft and 3x6 ft raised beds a couple years ago,  I wanted cattle panels running behind all of them.  It has worked out really well.  They stay up year round and since we are getting old, we appreciate that!  Now I can plant anything in whatever location we need. I love the idea of the tomato alley!  If it works well for you, you might consider just wiring the cattle panels on the T-posts and making them permanent. The post driver is my least favorite part of the job.  Your boys are going to be such smart, strong, healthy young men with great skills from growing up on your farm.  I look forward to seeing your videos each Saturday.  Blessings to you and your family.

text: When Kevin looks for a new job, sometimes stocking groceries in a family oriented grocery store can allow flex hours for when fatherhood starts calling.  You do usually start at a lower wage but advance in pay especially with unions.  You can sometimes work overnights - not the ideal situation - but it leaves you free for daytime duties and emergencies.  It makes interviews a little tricky but a manager with a family is going to be more understanding.  Best of luck with the job market!

author: @Gotofy105
text: What you just experienced, my friend, is something called "Refeeding Syndrome". Essentially, you went on such an intense diet that when you tried to eat a shit ton of food, your body completely lacked the energy to digest said food, so expelled it. Take this as a sign that doubling your calories is fine, but quadrupling is suicidal if you've just been on an almost week long fast.

author: @lilMissAdoria
text:
I know you probably know this, but find someone who sells farm fresh eggs in your area. 
For me, it's the lady who owns the garbage dump. She sells eggs on the side for about 4$ per dozen. They are the best eggs I've ever had the privilege of frying up. 
I save those eggs if I have store bought too, and use the store bought for scrambled or baking type cooking where the texture being loose isn't a problem, and save the farm ones for breakfast sandwiches, or hard boiling. It's crazy how much better they taste and hold together in a frying pan, and I get to support a local farm who loves their birds.

author: @gingercollier7949
text: I used to work for Nordstrom in the Visual Merchandising  department back in the day when it was window design and installation. I have reupholstered all kinds of chairs and pieces of wood. Here is what I would do. Staple the widest edge and use a chalk line to line up your edge to staple. Start in the middle go towards the end and pull your fabric tight, then staple. Staple half of one side, then the other always pulling your fabric tight. Start the opposite in the middle and do the same steps. It is the end that makes it messy. The thickness in the fold can be cut out. I like to do the ends like your gift wrapping.fold the sides in and staple just about and 1/8”-1/4” in from the edge.cut away that extra fabric , then fold in. Again go back to the middle and staple from the middle to the end pulling tightly as you staple. If you are doing something that is circular staple in the North, South, East, West spot of the circle pulling tightly across. Staple one quarter area but clip V off the fabrics so it lays flat. That is the fabric that will be lumpy. Do the opposite and pull the fabric. Always work opposite and pull the fabric tight.

author: @StudSupreme
text:
I know the Gros Ventre. In 1976, my dad took the whole family on a 5 day pack trip up into those mountains. It was a life changing event. It was from that event that I eventually understood that Wyoming is a legitimate argument for proof of the Divine. 
I was a hyper energetic, rambunctious kid of 14, growing up in NJ. Charlie Peterson, his son and his son's girlfriend took the family up into a forest along a winding trail, and we emerged in a great valley, gradually coming up on a line camp which consisted of an Indian style teepee, with at least 100 cows spread out over a pasture. You could hear the cows gently mooing over the valley, smell the splendidly clean mountain air, and see these massive mounds and peaks of granite in tremendous sharpness and clarity against an ENORMOUS blue sky. God, thank you for that gift.
We eventually stopped at a pre-established campsite. We spent the next several days riding up into different valleys, getting ice off a glacier, trout fishing and occasionally eating wild strawberries. One night, we saw a satellite traversing the night sky, and it was the first time I ever saw the Milky Way. On the second to last day, my horse got startled and threw me, and I broke my arm. But that only added to the tremendous adventure.
Thank you, God Almighty.

author: @lanaserenelife
text:
Hello all :)

About to go to sleep for the first time in over 35 hours after this lol 😴 but just wanted to clarify a few things!:

I was out until 1:15 AM driving my mom and sister to the airport. They are gone this weekend. I discovered I was locked out once I got home. 

Had they been home, I would've just drove straight there. 

I do not have a key to their home. This is the second time they're ever both gone away at the same time, so we've never thought to give me an official key because one of them is always there when I visit.

Why didn't you:

Go to a hotel? 
- I could not have imagined this would take 9 hours. I thought it would be done by 4 AM latest, they also kept promising someone would call. By the time I realized they weren't calling, it wasn't financially worth it, hotels in my area are around $200/night. I'd rather skip one night of sleep honestly, especially if I'm getting there at 5 AM.

Sleep in your parking garage?
- My parking garage is freezing cold, last month there was a break in where things got stolen, I had no blankets, and most of all: there's no cell phone service in an underground parking garage! I also know myself and my sleep habits and it's unlikely I'd even be able to fall asleep.

Stay in your lobby?
We are all different. Some people could've stayed there all night and felt good with that, I stayed in my lobby for 2 hours and couldn't take it anymore. I can't stay stagnant that long. I love driving, I say it all the time, night drives don't scare me, and it's safer to be moving in my opinion. Please also keep in mind I worked 2 AM closing shifts at the movie theater for almost 5 years, and have solo road tripped many states alone at night! While I'm cautious, my town does not scare me.

Thank you to the lovely people who have been so nice and helpful! It was a difficult night, but I'm grateful for you. Also very excited to go to sleep right now. Goodnight everyone ❤️
video_title: locked out of my apartment and no where to go (all nighter vlog)

author: @TheodoraWohler
text:
This just happened to me, I just moved in a couple of weeks ago.  I am not from this area and have no family close by 
I went to the Police Station because I just didn’t know what to do.  They were great!  They called the 24 hour emergency number after I tried several times.  
Interestingly enough the property manager was there within the hour. The police said to the manager, this was a dangerous situation.  If emergency services needed in the building and there was no access, the management company could be held accountable.   
The officer was filing 
an investigation report and a detective from the department would be following up with the management company.  
I was hesitant about going to the police but they said I did the right thing.

author: @debbieb2584
text:
It’s completely unacceptable for a property manager to handle a lockout situation this way. If a tenant had the ability to call a locksmith immediately because management took far too long to respond, then the property manager should absolutely reimburse and compensate them for that expense.

This isn’t just an inconvenience — it’s a serious safety issue. As others have pointed out, what if someone left the stove on, had a pet trapped inside, or there was another emergency situation? Tenants deserve timely assistance and professional communication, especially in situations that directly impact their safety and access to their home.
Glad you’re safe and sound!!

text: Don't worry if the agapanthus gets droopy. It's just transplant shock. Keep it well watered as it gets adjusted. author: @yaneseann
text: every couple of years, you’ll need to ‘thin’ your agapanthus to ensure they don’t get overcrowded which can cause them to stop blooming - aggy’s are pretty hardy so you can literally take a clean shovel and split the roots… i do this every 2-3 years for my garden when i thin my iris bulbs, and then you get more plants for free! 😊
video_title: transforming our run down garden beds *before & after* | MaCenna Lee

author: @tambij
text: Whenever I do transplants,  I always cut them down to about 4-6 inches of foliage. This will keep the energy of the plant focused on the roots to keep your plant healthy while it adjusts to the new area.  It’s not going to be beautiful this year. But next year it will have strong root system and  the plant  will be beautiful.  🌱   I  planted Peonies in my front yard about 4 years ago but they never flowered! So I transplanted them last year in early spring to my back yard because it gets morning & afternoon sunlight and they are so BIG about 3 1/2 feet tall and the blooms are at least 5 inches around, dark burgundy 🌺 I had no idea they were such a beautiful color. I’m so happy I transplanted them 👍
video_title: transforming our run down garden beds *before & after* | MaCenna Lee

How to get rid of black mold on wood 
Distilled white vinegar and 3% hydrogen peroxide kill black mold instantly on contact by penetrating porous wood fibers. Both neutralize spores without releasing toxic fumes. Avoid bleach; it only strips surface color, and its high water content can drive mold deeper into the wood

author: @JustyouraveragemattNZ
text:
Hello everyone, I am Matt from New Zealand! Thank you all for your beautiful comments!
Jonna, It was an absolute joy to meet you and your team and see your beautiful gallery! I can't really put into words just how much it meant to me. Sollefteå will absolutely be on my itinerary next time I come to Sweden.
What I forgot to say on camera was that I actually ended up needing a kidney transplant which was donated to me by my Mum. I must acknowledge her generosity in giving me back my life! I was watching another of Jonna's videos in my hospital bed after the transplant when I decided I was definitely going to visit the shop in Sollefteå.

I want to say just how beautiful this community of people is. Everyone I met at the gallery was so kind and friendly. This channel truly has the kindest fanbase. I actually had dinner with the family from Vancouver that night!

To anyone struggling with kidney disease or facing the prospect of a transplant... or you just want to say hi :)  feel free to reach out to me. I will be wishing the very best for you.
video_title: A Month of Doubt, Gratitude & Growth | Life in Northern Sweden

author: @yahoooo846
text:
Oh my dear Jonna,
There was a time when your videos were the first thing I would watch whenever you uploaded. Your words, your art, your way of seeing the world always brought me peace. But life became heavy, and I slowly lost my way. I fell behind on your videos because I was fighting battles of my own my fiancé and I parted ways, I struggled financially, and there were days when the emotional weight felt impossible to carry. I felt lost for a very long time.
Today, as I finally caught up with your journey, my heart broke for all that you've endured. I knew about Nanook, but I didn't know about you and Johan... didn't imagine the depth of the pain and loss you've been carrying. Watching how you've continued to move forward, showing up for your work, creating beauty, and carrying yourself with such grace through unimaginable grief, has given me more strength than I can put into words.
In a strange way, while I was trying to find my own light again, I found it reflected in your resilience. Your courage reminded me that even when life changes us forever, we can still keep going, one step at a time.
If you happen to read this, please know that you are deeply loved by people all around the world. You have touched hearts you may never meet, including mine. I am so proud of the woman you are, the strength you carry, the kindness you share, and the beauty you continue to create despite everything.
Please be gentle with yourself. Take care of your heart. You have given so much to others, and you deserve all the love, peace, and healing in return.
Sending you the biggest hug, endless love, and heartfelt gratitude.
With love,
A grateful sister from afar ❤️

author: @Samieseeker
text:
Jonna, forgive my bluntness,  but talk to your mother about your family menopause history.  Night time anxiety is a common symptom of early peri menopause.  I don't know your age but it can start young in many women.  I could be completely off target but if not, there are things you can do to ease symptoms. 
One thing that really helped me was to make a non-negotiable promise to myself to never worry about anything in the dark. 
Put on a talking book or something,  but just refuse to let your mind doom loop. 
Everything is worse in the dark. When the sun is up, you can make plans for what you can control and hug people for the things you can't control.  
Lots of love from Australia.

author: @starflower703
text:
I completely understand the melancholy that accompanies joy and rebirth in the Spring. It’s something I have experienced at varying levels every spring. This year has been incredibly intense. I think for me it’s been a combination of a very hard winter with some health challenges, the 5 year anniversary of my husband’s death, and the beginning of a new relationship last June. I have been processing all the changes in my life good and bad since loosing my husband in 2021 and my mother in 2022 just 6 months apart. 

My new love is so different than any relationship I’ve ever had; it’s the kind of love I have dreamed about my entire life and realizing what I missed out on for many years has made me incredibly sad and incredibly happy and grateful all at the same time. 

Your video helped me to put it into words. Thank you Jonna for always being so open with us. Your journey always touches me so deeply and for that I am grateful!
video_title: A Month of Doubt, Gratitude & Growth | Life in Northern Sweden

author: @MaddyVi70
text: Don’t be afraid Jonna, just breath and… let your light shine ✨ You’re a special creature 💖 5 years ago my mom felt pain in the belle and Medical e and discovered a pancreatic cancer. She had only a few months left. I didn’t tell her anything, I told she has a strong inflamnation and needed rest. I was in despair but one evening, watching one of your videos, I had the idea of creating a forest in her room. I put 3 of your paintings that I had bought and I spent all the time with her, laying together in her bed and watching all your videos from number one. She told me you gave her peace and called you “my Forest Angel”… The last video she watched before dying was the one where Sinaaq died and told us and celebrated her life in a part of the video. My mom told me: don’t cry, I will be running in the Forest with Sinaaq ❤️💙 when I hear the song you put in that moment of the video, I think about her, about you and about Sinaaq, and my heart is full of love snd gratitude for you, a person that I never Knew, but he lord me so much in the most difficult time of my life ❤️ so I wish to thank you so much Jonna… I had written you a letter and put in a package with a gift, it was a hand-made wood scarf with all the shades of green, but the package was lost in a internation custom center. I was very sorry. But I told myself that one day I will come to Solleftea and I will give another one to you with my hands. I’ve written this to you now to tell you how special you are 🌷 much love and a big hug 🤗 Maddy from Italy

text: I’m a 40 year old disabled woman. I get a set amount of money each month, and a set amount of “help” from my Disability. Things were already “snug” and in anticipation of things getting tighter, I’ve gotten used to eating less. We’ve started dropping portion sizes a little at a time. We have one “cooked” meal a day. I eat a lot of shelf-stable TV dinners. Snacks are applesauce packets, fruit cups, or snacks bars. We can afford a few “goodies” at the store, but we keep it tight - and I’m looking to tighten it a little more. I resent that I measure every item that goes into my grocery cart - but with prices this high? I can’t afford to dip into savings every month to afford that “dinner” I want to fix. author: @easyplease5068
text:
I am so relieved to see a video like this surfacing. Your lilac pruning video is like gold to me. Seeing you now warms my heart.

You get what's going on.

I've been tracking the progress and scope of this coming El Nino for months now as well as fertilizer ingredients not getting through the Strait of Hormuz and the other things you mention and their inevidable impacts on the food system to name but one system.
 
Some climate aware friends and I have started bulk buying dry ingredients together through a wholesaler (I have a business account thank god) and have had been having serious conversations about what collapse could be like and also how to mitigate the temptation some will feel to go full Walking Dead right off the bat.

I like the tone you strike here. 
Helping the vulnerable. Helping each other, being friends, having fun while processing adrenaline pinching stuff...People who are mentally and emotionally unprepared will freak out in all liklihood and will need support and a safe place to talk rather than act out. 
We can be safe harbour for each other.

Being aware that some people will try to steal our harvests if push comes to shove, is just being realistic. We're trying to wrap our heads creatively around that one. Storing dry goods in camoflaged, rodent proof metal garbage cans and in several different places incase of theft in one spot, is one.

I've learned to make 100% sourdough bread from organic ancient grains. If stored properly, the grain can last 20 years so stockpiling is a good idea. The grains can be cooked like rice as well. They are highly nutritious and the bread is very sustaining. Its so nutritious infact that loaves could become barter items.

How to handle people you know and care about showing up on your door because they know you have food but don't have resources themselves? Barter. They can work or figure out something they can offer to help the whole.

We're Canadian, not in the cauldron of the shit show that is Trump et al's America. We watch in utter horror and deep compassion to what is happening to your country. We have our own incredible dysfunction here as well and are of course equally treated like slaves by the ruling class who give zero fucks about any of us. We are stupid sheep to them and they fleece us whenever they desire.

Anyhow, listening to others helps broaden my scope in comprehending what all is coming down the pike.
I hear you.
I see you and am with you lady.

Gah, sorry this is so long but sharing ideas is so important right now.

author: @NurseCyn
text: Definitely agree with other posters to look into the lemon laws in your state. I was able to threaten a lemon to Mercedes-Benz after a custom car that I got was in the shop for almost 2 months for different things that started that night. I took the car home. I contacted Mercedes-Benz add the suggestion of one of the salesman that I’m familiar with at the dealership. I did tell them that I was thinking of contacting a lemon law attorney to lemon my car. They returned all of the payments back to me plus the oil changes and the down payment. I decided to steer away from luxury cars after having Mercedes for several years and bought a Honda CRV 2025 hybrid. It’s the best decision I ever made. I love this car and I don’t miss the Mercedes at all. Definitely google lemon law in Delaware. I’m in California so every state is different. California is pretty generous and has more protections for consumers than for dealers.

author: @SavageElf82
text: I do enjoy these videos so much but ofcourse they are new start ups for the most part. I'd love to see a year 3-ish gardener who has been moderately successful on their own but is battling issues that make you want to pull your hair out. Like slugs! Maybe go twice to show seasonal change ups for multi-seasonal growers in the lower states. Id love to have year round gardening . Or how to get something other than hot peppers when your temps are 100+. My seasons have been done by late june, everything gets baked and then gets blight. Rotating crops when you use raised beds. Controlling an over grown herb bed. Battling season changes from cold to rain to sweltering heat. I personally feel like ive learned maybe too much and now 3 years in im kind of lost but I still celebrate every undersized tomato and my hens enjoy every vegetable the pests get to before I do. Im trying so hard to grow organic and not use pesticides but I think I feed the neighborhood insects more than I feed my family. Lol 😅 maybe some ideas to mull over. Also one more: maybe untraditional methods for budget or small space gardeners. Like example: ive seen grapes growing on all kinds of trellises but info on training them. Like I have my grape vines on an arch trellis and because its not traditional I cant find pruning advice. I dont have the ability to run 10 ft vines along the fence so I have to make my garden space compact with variety. Id also love more advice on trap plants. Where to put them, how far away, how to keep it going, types of plants to use. I use sunflowers and tomatillo to keep leaffooted bugs away from my tomatoes but its a small space and eventually everything gets nailed by those gross sap suckers as the heat goes up. Anyway. Hope the ideas help and keep making the great content you guys do. I enjoy all the epic channels. Thanks.

author: @sunshine.8197
text:
GMO stands for "genetically modified organism." Meaning the plant/animal was purposefully genetically changed in some way, such as dogs being selectively bred for good temperament (even back when they were just starting to be domesticated) or corn plants propagated from individuals with more cornels per ear. GMO also includes modern methods like genetic engineering where specific genes can be spliced in at the microscopic level.

Anything that claims it has "non-GMO corn" is lying. Modern corn plants are not the same corn as 1000 years ago, and that's because of humans' selective planting. Maybe there's some older types of corn out there, but even then, they would have been continually propagated specifically for resemblance to ancient corn. 

There was actually huge progress made in drought and insect resistant corn some years ago. These qualities were from the addition of specific genes. It was done in order for commercial farmers to lose less crop and help continue to feed people at affordable prices. There are definitely grey areas in the world of genetic modification, but the point is that **modification happens at many levels and is not inherently dangerous or evil.**

The rise of "non GMO" labels and marketing surrounding that are due to a lot of fear mongering and misinformation spread mainly in the 2010s/teens. Because so many people thought GMO = bad and refused to learn anything beyond, companies adapted their marketing. Like how some things now say "gluten free" even if it's a beverage or other inherently gluten free food. Labeling on things like pasta or crackers are helpful because those are typically glutenous and it helps people with celiac identify safe foods, but some labels truly are just to appeal to people following trends.

author: @claudinestone6152
text: Eds are diagnosed as a mental illness so physically the illness affects and looks differently. That guy is really hurting because it DOES steal your physical ability as it progresses and with time. It WAS affecting you and Im thankful people saw this and forced you into treatment. You managed to turn it around, which is amazing, before it took your ability to run. You were going down that slippery slope so Im very thankful that it didnt harm you to the point where you lost so much muscle and bone that it took away your love, maybe permanently, or lead to death. It’s what made me change. If you cant pick up your legs walking as they feel like bricks, I literally wanted to die. I am now running again! That has been my biggest motivation. But for many years I could run and ran for college, until I couldnt and lost my scholarship. I hope his daughter recovers and is inspired by your recovery! Im sorry he made that comment but hes hurt at what this disease took from his daughter. He is also probably jealous as you are living the LIFE now and sure he wishes his daughter was the same. But there are many anorexics that function well and many that cant. But over time it takes. The people I know that died from EDs were mostly normal weight actually. So seriousness of the disease cant always be seen.

author: @deborahsarna2300
text: So I know you are thinking about the house situation in terms of potentially buying something smaller. Let me just toss out this idea (from personal experience with my two boys-- who are now adults). You have three active boys who are going to grow into phases of hanging with friends. The spaces you have now that you see as not used much actually can take on a different purpose for your boys and their friends. I was a mom who LOVED having the boy energy and friends fill up the house. I also liked knowing they could be hanging with their friends in OUR house, where they could be comfortable and safe-- particularly into the teenage years. We hosted lots of sleepover situations because I had a rule: once it got to 11 pm/midnight, everyone was "in for the night." No driving when they were tired, etc. Motivation to stick around = pancake breakfast in the morning. So the "unused" space became "crash on the sofa" or inflate a bed/toss some pillows and blankets on the floor. My boys and their friends always knew they were welcome/at home and there was always a place for everyone to hang. Just give it some thought. ❤ ❤❤

text:
It's disgusting how expensive basic human needs can be... I wish you all the best


I find it silly that people are saying this isn't a basic human need...? Like those are her nutrients, her food and water. Humans need that to survive otherwise they'd die. She has a condition that doesn't make her consume things normally which is why she spends this crazy amount of money just to survive. Her nutrients shouldn't be this expensive because she has a health condition that she can't reverse. If someone had her same condition and wasn't fortunate enough to able to afford that then they'd die.

author: @Caylarion
text:
It's a good thing she keeps putting on hand sanitizer, cause if any unknown germ gets into the supplies that she handles by her hands, that too could be life threatening...

My cousin had to do this too, he used so many steps to keep his supplies clean, and to keep his hands very clean as well, in order to safely deliver his pouches to his stomach without it becoming life threatening...

Everyone's special in their own way, and for this lady to be so strong & happy each day, makes me smile to see that she's doing okay

She's truly an inspiration, and to those who are also suffering from this, whoever you are, don't give up the fight, your strong, kind, and have a heart of gold, cause your worth living for🥰❤️‍🔥💐💐🌹💐

author: @sarahm3282
text:
As someone with an autoimmune disease living in the us, its difficult to see these prices as anything but eugenics. 
Our gov could support production costs. They could put limits on prices and just make everything affordable. They could regulate health insurance and prosecute companies that refused to cover lifesaving treatments.

author: @PinkJG619
text: We have snakes, hawks, crows, wild cats, opossums and raccoons.  We have chicken wire all around the sides, and screen nets on the top, there is an extra layer on apart of the coop to offer more shade if wanted, chickens love music and mirrors and things to perch on and they like to scratch and dust bathe. We have never had an issue with with any animals getting in our coop from any outside way of the coop… only in the way that WE open it for the eggs. My friend had a coop where she pulls the drawer out to collect the eggs and all her chickens disappeared and when she checked her cameras raccoons every night helped themselves to chickens. We have it where we lift the roof and can take out the eggs. We had a raccoon take one chicken so we added a latch lock. We did have one instance where a baby opossum got in but that was during a coop clean out and it snuck in there lol. We had 5 chickens at first, one was eaten 2 years later, then one died and one became a bully and ate the other ones. We got 2 more and we are seeing how it goes because we want more but not if she’s gonna keep eating them. We are doing slow introduction.

author: @cactuskid1492
text: Oh boy! Agreed that trying to make this a super long road trip would have been hard. You can only stretch out 2500 miles for so long, but if you just give us a video of each day… that could end up being several months of content if you did one video a week. You have your plan. Do it. Was exciting to see you on my side of the US. California is a wonderful place to start or end a journey. I love that your dad will be with you the entire time and you’ll be leaving a piece him along this historic route. I just lost my mom (my favorite person) last month and I, like you, know the grief will come when it decides to come. There is no stopping it. I wouldn’t want to. I let it wash over me like a cold Pacific wave. Yes, the Pacific is cold. Basically all year. It’s never warm. If that happens… we’re all f**ked. Safe travels, Gina.

author: @mikeperez8628
text: So, this is a great video and offers several things to consider. I think we are in camp three as far as weight is concerned: knowing what your truck can safely pull as well as the payload and cargo capacities, and then choosing an RV, or conversely, knowing the gross weight and pin weight of your RV and choosing a truck to more than match. We were blessed to be in a position to do both (F350 SD dually pulling a 2022 Northpoint 310) - what a learning curve, and still learning! I suggest you also need to know your truck's cargo capacity (what is in the bed) and your payload (max it can haul - you, fuel, tool box, etc.) especially as your pin weight is cargo. As someone mentioned in another comment, the yellow sticker on your doorjamb will give you what you need to know. Your tow/rear axle weight measured with the RV, minus your rear axle weight measured with your truck bed empty, is your actual cargo weight. Moving things from the RV to the truck will not help overall GCRW. Safe travels, love the videos!

author: @theoutsiders7318
text: I'm in the camp of Be in Spec! With everything that will be riding in the tow vehicle and trailer weighed all at one time. Full fuel cell as well, water, LP, etc. If you are involved in an accident a lawyer will pick those numbers apart and you could be held at fault for that reason. We have an older Airstream that needed axles. I went as high as I could on weight rating for the replacements. From 3200# to 3800#, went from 10" brakes to 12" brakes and kept the 6 lug wheels. Our rig rides so good and smooth now. Best upgrade you can do to an Airstream.

author: @oldguy1030
text:
I'm glad you are OK with it.

I have yet to weigh my truck and trailer combo.  But then, given that circumstances have meant we've not gone anywhere with the trailer in over a year that's not too surprising.

Weighing will get done but the nearest CAT scale is more than 30 minutes away so you first have to tow in a potentially unsafe manner to find out if you are safe to tow.  Doesn't make too much sense.  I'll eventually put out for a tire/wheel scale and get good information in detail although that will be a slow process.  I already have a hitch scale.

Overall I'm not too worried.  I've a 1-ton truck and a travel trailer that isn't going to top 7200 pounds - ever.  The biggest concern is actually the stiff suspension with a relatively light trailer and that is why I got a Shocker hitch.

text: As some have already said. Weight is weight with the exception of payload and axle weight. Those items matter as much as pulling weight. Gvrw is only part of the equation. Check the sticker with your tire size and air pressure on the door jam. It will tell you your payload. That is the max your truck can carry.

But out in my garden which I didn't get to put in till May, it started off good and now that we've had so much rain, I've lost sections of it. I had to replant my squash and zucchini. My pinto beans got hit hard by all the rain and I've lost 75 percent of my rows. But my purple hull peas are doing good and my okra. My potatoes are a little spotty, some rotted from the rain. author: @kimireneanderson9645
text:
So, true what she's saying.  I've been listening to the weather scientists talk about the weirdness.  Things I've never heard about before like Rosby waves, Omega Blocks, weakening Westerly winds and all this is happening before the Super El Nino that's got these scientists worried.  

I live in a 3rd floor  Philadelphia condominium and am growing potatoes, tomatoes, bell peppers and strawberries in my windows and augmenting with grow lamp strips.  With temperatures being 97 degrees on day then 60 the next, I'm glad I'm growing indoors.  I've also been able to "can" some bell peppers, and stocking up on canning jars.

I also have hydroponic setups but haven't started to use them yet.

Even if you fail, it's still good to get into the practice of gardening.

author: @TediumGenius
text: First video of yours I've seen, and you've got great advice to give, I totally agree with everything you've said!  In Central PA for the past few years it's almost as if the seasons shifted: summer into September or November, and flash cold much later into the spring.  I know we're at a high solar cycle and are headed to less solar activity for the next half cycle.  I think it is either 11 or 19 years... but in any case you'll have to plant different varieties, be ready with late starts planted by seed just in case crops do poorly, and make all kinds of contingency plans.  At least that's how I see it, as a lot has changed with my vegetables, fruits, and ornamental landscape in the last three years or so.  I was blessed with tomato, bean, pea, and a few other compost pile volunteers, and if it weren't for those, we would have missed out on two tomato types, one type of pepper, and a full crop of beans, because these were bug infested due to planting timing I think.  God bless, and I subscribed!

author: @jeffreygunn3530
text: Telluride is great (but expensive). Before driving down there in the summer, though, check the calendar. They have several music festivals there, and it'll be super crowded on those weekends. Also, don't try to drive into Telluride itself. Drive to the nearby "suburb" of Mountain Village, park in the garage, and take the free gondola that goes into Telluride. Telluride is a 19th century mining town, and there is very little parking for visitors.

text: I have found their cruise directors understand the product and their clients, which is a big plus for me. They have felt very present when compared to other cruises I have been on; I love that they were the first person I saw getting onto the ship and the last person to wave me goodbye at disembarkation. Perhaps a silly observation, but I think it is a clever way of setting the scene for a personalised and relationship-based experience.
video_title: I Keep Returning To Holland America Despite These Problems. Here's Why

author: @thinking4902

video_title: I Keep Returning To Holland America Despite These Problems. Here's Why

author: @wtaffle
text: Just off the Westerdam north Pacific crossing.  We were pleasantly surprised to have some amazing singer/dance shows from the World Stage Cast.  We were told HAL is working with a new agent and this pilot project was on Westerdam,  and soon to be rolled out to Nieuw Amsterdam.   While not the big Broadway shows of some other lines, it was a huge improvement in stage show entertainment!   The singers were some of the best I've heard on any cruise line.  I made sure to include my positive comments on my post cruise survey.   Let's hope HAL continues to expand this new program across the rest of the fleet!
video_title: I Keep Returning To Holland America Despite These Problems. Here's Why

author: @whealkenn
text: The local fish program was great when we were on a European cruise with HAL.  We also love the way that they run their salad bar.  The music venues are a huge hit for us.  We always choose HAL when it is an option.  I do wish they allowed us to do our own laundry.
video_title: I Keep Returning To Holland America Despite These Problems. Here's Why

text: Hello, I have taken 24 HAL cruises and have two more scheduled for later in the year. Why do I cruise HAL?  Because I like the ship sizes, room and dining services and most of all the friendliness of the crew.  I am now in my early 60's and enjoy traveling with people in my age group, no hate of children but I do not want to deal with a large number on a cruise.  I have spoken to other solo travelers, and we are excited with Evaluation which will take into account solo travelers in the future. The only thing that I find lacking is the entertainment on the main stage.

text:
We really enjoy the quiet, as well. 
They are evolving the main stage shows back to more singing, and singing and dancing together. Which is what I remember from before the shutdown. 
I’m happy that they are bringing Grand Dutch Cafe to the six Vista and Signature class ships. The new cabins sound interesting, but some, like the Bridge View Suite, are going to take away from something. Same for Grand Dutch. You don’t get to add things without making the ship bigger, which is not happening, or taking something away. So I am excited, but a bit worried as well. If the update to all the cabins gets rid of the shorter curtains, I will be overjoyed!

text: Your dark chocolate appreciation reminded me of a fond childhood memory. I grew up in my grandma's dance studio, so to speak, that was about 30 minutes away from my home.  .. traveled there a few times a week from the time I was 3 years old. There was a "Mall", this was in the early 60's, near by. About once a month my parents would let me go to a candy store in the Mall , on the way home. I would save up my money, which might be a dollar or two, and ask to buy a chunk of dark chocolate for that amount. 🥰 And I would eat and savor that "chunk" of dark chocolate until my next chance to visit that store again. 😊 Thank you for bringing that precious memory back to me. 🤗❤️❤️❤️

author: @trishsmith552
text: Katie, I am an old woman. In my day (the 60s) we had Home Economics classes in middle school and high school. The metal utensil you used to spread the frosting on the fruit pizza was called a spatula (not the things used to flip food in the pan). I think those were called turners. About the only thing the spatula was used for was to level off measuring cups. In cake making there is a version of that called an offset spatula, used for spreading frosting. That's what you really needed for your pizza. It would keep your knuckles out of the frosting. And I'm with you girl. I can't roll a crust out round to save my life. You should see my flour tortillas lol

author: @al1ce_rabb1t
text: So, my best guess for the Anker powerbank and cable is that the break in the cable caused a voltage spike somewhere and in your and Luke's laptops fried the PowerDelivery controller(s) in your MacBooks. When no PD negotiation takes place, for any charger+device combo with USB-C, it'd function the same as charging over USB-A, on 5V and about 2 or 3A at most, which means 10-15W at best. For a MacBook Neo or M1 Air, that's kind of enough for slow but regular charging. But for anything more power hungry, like 14 or 16 inch MacBook Pro's, that only amounts to trickle charging. It sounds like in the case of your MacBook in particular, it damaged more than just the PD controller, but probably also further power management inhibiting the laptop from engaging your performance cores and GPU cores or simply power-throttling them severely.

author: @bellsdawne
text: So I am in dental hygiene school and one of my teachers is a myofunctional therapist. The claim that expanding your palate improves health isn’t completely false; however, everything I’ve been taught has shown promise in GROWING palates. If you use a removal palate widener from the ages 18m-12 years, it helps widen your airway and reduce the risk of developing sleep disordered breathing as an adult. BUT these are new studies and in adults it’s not the same bc the bones in your palate are fused. Orthodontia has a reputation of being much more painful and extensive in adults because the bone is more dense. I don’t know if this matters at all but it’s weird to see how they turned an emerging preventative therapy into red-pill content😢

author: @JayeCole
text:
FYI / clarification - GCSEs are not like the SATs and do not get you entrance into a university.

We have a different school system and secondary schools here in the UK are for ages 11-16 and end in exams which give you a “qualification”. Typically pupils will want at least 7 but sometimes they will get as many as 9 GCSE qualifications. 

From 16-18 students attend Sixth Form College which ends with exams called A-levels. These are more specialised and most students will get 3 and sometimes 4 of these qualifications. These are used to apply for university.

So her kids are not only not getting the equivalent of a high school diploma, but they aren’t even getting a middle school-ish level of education.

Without GCSEs it will be nearly impossible to get employment anywhere.

author: @mikeyjoshua1846
text:
I think I felt my last two brain cells die at the "radiation makes sense of taste worse" thing. He's so confidently wrong. Taste diminishes because of lower air pressure and also because air in planes is usually quite dry which affects your sense of smell which in turn affects your sense of taste.
Also, as a dental assistant in an orthodontic practice, your resting tongue position CAN make a difference in your teeth's positions but it will not change your facial structure especially if you're a grown ass dude. The sutures in the skull harden by about age 14-16 and trying to expand the palate at that point is a pain in the ass.

author: @clownd0ll_
text: Unschooling infuriates me. I’ve seen a lot of it on social media and it seems to boil down to parents being disinterested in being active parents, realizing that doing nothing is easier than taking an active role in their children’s education. All the things they point out that they do in unschooling are things that all parents should be doing on TOP of traditional schooling. Grocery shopping, budgeting, exposure to nature, travelling, reading with your children, helping with homework outside of school. These are all bare minimum and this whole movement exposes the fact that these parents were not doing these things in the first place. It’s child neglect. It’s abuse.

author: @lynnboyett5540
text:
Why am I gardening?
When I was thirteen, I listened to my grandfather talk about the Depression. He was a prolific gardener with a large, bountiful garden every year. I realized the cycle of circumstances and that I would probably face a “depression” in my lifetime. I’ve prepared.
Also, I’m been financially wiped out, to zero, three times for different reasons each time. I have the experience of knowing that all of it can happen to me even with the best of plans.
Now, the prices in my retirement years are rising very fast for a fixed income. I’m glad I’ve invested time in learning gardening early because the learning curve would have put me behind otherwise.
Look, if I can learn pressure canning at 64, anyone can.
video_title: Food Prices Are About to Surge… Start Growing NOW

author: @judymadge7347
text: I recently moved house.  We have 3 veg patches that hadn't been used in years and were desperate for nutrients! I've turned the soil, fertilised and sown some veg even here in Melbourne!  I got a worm farm for Mother's Day and am setting up more composting areas.  I'm watching the sun and shade to determine what to plant where, but, in the meantime, I've planted some peas, garlic, potatoes, broccoli, onions, lettuce and some flowers.  I'm growing my own veg as I question the nutritional value of today's veg in the supermarkets and I also do not want to pay such ridiculous prices!  And finally, I find a deep sense of peace when I'm growing my own food!  Dirty fingernails = my happy place!

text:
I grow food because it's one of the most satisfying things in life for me. I grew a 10 kilo pumpkin last year and it was my first ever, I've never felt as proud with anything I've ever done in my life. I'd rather do that than sit in an office cubicle for the next 30 years.
Granted, I still need to sit there for a while, because food's not the only thing that costs in life. But damn, doing that with no plan to stop until retirement is such a meaningless existence.
video_title: Food Prices Are About to Surge… Start Growing NOW

author: @CBEEBLE
text:
What happened to the planters/furniture (assuming paid for with communal funds) on the roof terrace? All tenants/residents had keys which strongly suggests that access was not accidental or unauthorised. Even if the lease does not expressly grant access, the terrace appears to have been treated as an established amenity of the building rather than a private or restricted area.
You should write collectively to the managing agent and ask them to confirm who authorised the change of locks, what happened to the planters/furniture and the basis relied upon for withdrawing access from residents and leaseholders without consultation. They should also be asked to confirm whether the withdrawal of access is connected in any way to any proposed roof, airspace or upward development, or any intention by the freeholder to preserve or assert development rights over the roof area.
Unfortunately, if the lease does not explicitly grant roof access and access has merely been permitted historically, the position may be less straightforward. However, the fact that residents were given keys and the terrace appears to have been used as a shared amenity means it is entirely reasonable to ask for a clear explanation before accepting that access can simply be withdrawn.

También estuve en A Coruña durante un año pero soy ciudadano americano y puedo ser tu abuelo. Quiero decirte que no conozco tus planes con el canal pero España tiene muchas posibilidades para ampliar un canal mostrando pueblos y sitios con distintas peculiaridades como por ejemplo las aguas termales de Galicia o las aguas termales de Lanjaron en Granada, Andalucía dónde se han desarrollado balnearios y embotelladoras. Esto podría ser interesante para el desarrollo de tu canal y provechoso para las empresas que operan en esos lugares por el gran número de seguidores que tienes.
